v0.1.14 — SDD pipeline hardening + prompt engineering (R1-R5)

Prompt engineering:
- R1: pipeline context in all 7 personas (phase position + upstream/downstream)
- R2: auto-check "Before finalizing:" added to REASONING of all 7 personas
- R3: DevOps ROLE strengthened — final gate framing, dishonesty consequence
- R4: few-shot ❌/✅ examples in PM and Developer
- R5: positive constraints ("Always X") in all 7 personas

SDD pipeline:
- extractRequirements() propagates no_go_zone, user_personas, user_stories (with concrete AC) to all downstream phases
- Human Review Checklists at end of all 5 core phase briefings
- OPTIONAL_PHASES extracted to SSoT in lib/phases/index.js (was duplicated in 5 files)
- phase4: removed dead code (qualDebt fr_type filter), head(120)→head(200) for system design

Tests: 135 total (+32 from context.test.js + phase checklist tests)

commit.message:
fix: convert postcss.config.ts to .js so PostCSS natively resolves Tailwind plugin

PostCSS does not natively support TypeScript config files. The .ts extension
was likely causing Railway builds to skip Tailwind processing, producing a
minimal CSS bundle without utility class definitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: full hardening pass — rate limits, sanitisation, file upload, env validation

Section 1: app.disable('x-powered-by') — remove Express fingerprint
Section 2: rate limiters updated to 15-min windows; dedicated aiLimiter (1hr/5)
           applied to /parse-resume and /resume/ai-rewrite before general limiter
Section 3: JWT_SECRET length >= 32 enforced at startup; no weak fallback exists
Section 4: sanitize-html installed; sanitize() helper added to candidate.js and
           employer.js; applied to all textarea/freeform text fields in PUT handlers
           and work experience repeater fields
Section 5: SQL injection audit — all queries use parameterised pool.execute(); no changes needed
Section 6: /dpr/:postId/resume ownership verified via getProfileId(req.user.user_id)
           (already present); confirmed all profile routes tied to JWT user_id
Section 7: magic-login and accept-invite SQL now filter AND used_at IS NULL AND
           expires_at > NOW() — DB enforces expiry, not just application code
Section 8: /dpr/:postId/resume now checks RESUME_ALLOWED_TYPES (PDF/DOC/DOCX);
           filename sanitized (strip non-alphanumeric chars) in both resume routes
Section 9: config/db.js connectionLimit 10→20; connectTimeout: 10000 added;
           DB indexes added in initDB() for otp_codes, magic_links, employer_team
Section 10: REQUIRED_ENV startup check (DB_HOST/NAME/USER/PASSWORD + JWT_SECRET);
            process.exit(1) if any missing or JWT_SECRET < 32 chars
Section 11: global error handler added (4-arg Express middleware) after 404 route;
            all existing catch blocks already use generic messages — no stack leaks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Change deploy to accept artifact directory, move exec/public paths to app config

Deploy now takes a directory argument instead of --binary/--public flags. The
entire directory is tar.gz'd and deployed, allowing extra files (templates,
config, data) alongside the executable. --exec-path and --public-path are now
app-level settings on `app init` and `app set`, stored in verna.json.

commit.message:
Add subpart decomposition entry to lab notebook (2026-03-11)

New bottom entry. User thought verbatim + extended question table with
v1/v2 AND-condition subparts for each hard question. Clarifies AND/OR
is intra-question decomposition not cross-question merging. Notes on
retraining direction and interpretational output goal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: FilterPipeline per-group mode + direction awareness (#59)

* feat: FilterPipeline per-group mode + direction awareness (#59)

* fix: update existing tests to set blocked=True for block mode expectations (#59)

All existing tests expected block mode behavior but mocked FilterResult
without the blocked=True flag. The new proxy code checks filter_result.blocked
to determine block mode, so mocks must set this field appropriately.

Changes:
- test_filter_three_mode.py: test_block_mode_raises_error now sets blocked=True
- test_proxy.py: test_call_tool_blocks_when_filter_triggered now sets blocked=True
- test_proxy_filter.py: All block mode tests (4 tests) now set blocked=True

All 339 unit tests pass with 91.36% coverage (exceeds 90% floor).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: test_original_content_preserved_in_filter_result uses warn mode (#59)

original_content is only preserved in warn/block mode by design —
_apply_mode clears it in filter mode since content is already redacted.
Update test to pass group_settings={'mode': 'warn'} to match the intent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: dev data dir, env vars, editor save, bottom panel fixes (#42)

* chore: use ~/.mainframe_dev data dir for development

Separate dev data directory from production (~/.mainframe) to prevent
interference when running both simultaneously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update MAINFRAME_DATA_DIR to use absolute path in launch.json

* fix(desktop): bottom panel not opening from LeftRail play button

The Play button only toggled panelVisible but never cleared
panelCollapsed.bottom (default: true), so clicking it had no effect
until a launch config run happened to clear both flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(desktop): resolve user's login-shell PATH for launch configs

The packaged Electron app inherits a minimal PATH that doesn't include
nvm/fnm-managed node/npm. Spawn the user's login shell to capture the
full PATH instead of hardcoding known directories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: use DAEMON_PORT and VITE_PORT env vars, env overrides config.json

- Daemon reads DAEMON_PORT (no fallback to PORT) for its server port
- Vite reads VITE_PORT (no fallback to PORT) for the dev server port
- Config precedence: env vars > config.json > hardcoded defaults
- Documented all env vars in README, CLAUDE.md, and Developer Guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(desktop): filter WS events by active project to prevent cross-project leaks

chat.created, chat.updated, and sessions.external.count events from the
daemon were applied unconditionally to the Zustand store, causing sessions
from other projects to appear in the current project's session list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump version to 0.2.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add version bumping instructions to developer guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): resolve login-shell PATH at daemon startup

The packaged Electron app passes a resolved PATH to the daemon, but if
that resolution fails the daemon inherits a minimal PATH missing tools
like npm/pnpm installed via nvm/fnm. Resolve the user's login-shell
PATH directly in the daemon so it works regardless of how it was started.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core, desktop): use `-lic` to resolve login-shell PATH

Switch to using the `-lic` flags when resolving the user's login-shell PATH to ensure the full environment is loaded. This fixes potential issues with incomplete PATH resolution in both desktop and core modules.

* feat(desktop): add file save support to editor (CMD+S + save button)

- Add PUT /api/projects/:id/files endpoint for writing file content
- Add saveFileContent() client API function
- Wire EditorTab with onChange, dirty tracking, CMD+S keybinding, and
  a save button that appears when the file has unsaved changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add timeout guards to SWE-bench Pro test.sh verifiers

Jest/npm/pytest/go test commands in 5 task verifiers had no timeout,
causing indefinite hangs when individual tests stall (observed:
InteractiveAuthDialog-test.ts hung >2h in element-web task).

Adds `timeout 600` (10min) wrapper to all affected test runners.
Also adds `--forceExit` to jest commands to kill workers immediately
when tests complete (prevents jest from waiting for open handles).

Affected tasks:
- element-web-roomheaderbuttons-can-crash-fix-001 (jest)
- element-web-unread-indicators-diverge-fix-001 (jest)
- vscode-stale-diagnostics-feat-001 (npm test)
- ansible-abc-imports-fix-001 (pytest)
- teleport-users-can-delete-fix-001 (go test)

fix: extract tenant scope from event annotations instead of hardcoding platform

The activity processor was ignoring scope annotations on Kubernetes events,
causing all event-sourced activities to be attributed to the platform scope
regardless of which project control plane emitted them.

Both BuildFromEvent and buildActivity now call ExtractTenantFromAnnotations
to read the platform.miloapis.com/scope.type and scope.name annotations
that Milo's events proxy injects based on the authenticated user's context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix dashboard loading for existing users from landing page links

Issue:
- Landing page detection code was running on every render
- This caused dashboard to not load properly for existing users

Fix:
- Move fromLandingPage to lazy-initialized state (checks only once)
- Move landing page handling into useEffect (runs only once on mount)
- Use React Router's navigate() instead of window.history.replaceState()

This ensures existing users coming from /?from=landing-page get proper
dashboard loading without re-render issues.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Bug fixes from code review:

- Removed unnecessary useState in Ticker
- Fixed animation fill-mode for slide-up
- Responsive fixes: company header, metrics grid, admin table, 52W range

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[consensus] Remove randomness fast path code (#18870)

* [consensus] Remove randomness fast path

The randomness fast path used a separate, higher secrecy threshold
(via ConfigV2) for faster randomness reconstruction. It was disabled
by default and the mainnet on-chain config was reverted from V2 to V1.
This removes all fast path runtime code while preserving backward
compatibility with BCS-serialized on-chain and local DB state.

Key decisions:
- Transcripts.fast and AugmentedData.fast_delta Option fields are
  retained (always None) for BCS deserialization compatibility
- Move ConfigV2 struct is kept (published on-chain type) but new_v2()
  is removed
- Key pair deserialization handles old format gracefully by falling
  through to regenerate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix formatting and rebuild cached packages

Apply cargo +nightly fmt and rebuild aptos-cached-packages after
removing new_v2() from randomness_config.move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Return error on key pair deserialization failure instead of regenerating

Regenerating a new key pair on deserialization failure could cause a
mismatch with stale CertifiedAugData already persisted in storage,
leading to incorrect randomness shares. Instead, return an error to
disable randomness for the epoch, which is the safer behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep old key pair format for DB backward compatibility

Instead of migrating to a new serialization format, keep the old
(AugKeyPair, Option<AugKeyPair>) format and always write None for
the fast path key pair. This avoids any migration complexity and
ensures existing DB entries remain readable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: keep rounding and Move code unchanged for safety

Per review feedback:
- Restore rounding code to original (pass None for fast path threshold
  instead of removing the parameter) to ensure replay safety since
  rounding is called inside VM during DKG transaction verification.
- Restore new_v2() in randomness_config.move since it's a published
  public function on the framework.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep DKGPvssConfig.fast_wconfig for BCS backward compatibility

Retain the fast_wconfig: Option<SSConfig> field in DKGPvssConfig
(always set to None) to preserve BCS serialization compatibility,
consistent with the approach taken for Transcripts.fast and
AugmentedData.fast_delta.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Fix tests from recent rebase that still referenced removed FastShare
type and old 4-argument verify() signature. Remove the fast share
sender verification test (no longer applicable) and update remaining
tests to use the new 3-argument verify().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in DKGPvssConfig PartialEq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Keep ConfigV2 in OnChainRandomnessConfig enum for replay safety.
Historical DKG transactions may have used V2, and removing it from
TryFrom causes randomness_config_derived() to return None, which
would produce different DKGRounding parameters and break transcript
verification during blockchain replay.

V2 is now treated identically to V1: its secrecy_threshold and
reconstruction_threshold are used, but fast_path_secrecy_threshold
is ignored (fast path has been removed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Retain fast path secrecy threshold for rounding replay safety

Thread the fast_path_secrecy_threshold from on-chain ConfigV2 through
to DKGRounding::new, so that replay of historical V2-era DKG
transactions produces identical rounding parameters. For V1/Off
configs, None is passed (no fast path), preserving current behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Pass fast_wconfig from DKGRounding to DKGPvssConfig

Thread the fast_wconfig produced by DKGRounding through to
DKGPvssConfig instead of always using None. This ensures that
during replay of V2-era epochs, the DKGPvssConfig is constructed
with the same fast_wconfig as the original, matching the on-chain
BCS-serialized config exactly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Restore release builder V2 config and new_v2 constructor

Revert the removal of V2 variant from ReleaseFriendlyRandomnessConfig
and restore OnChainRandomnessConfig::new_v2() constructor. These are
needed for backward compatibility — removing them could break framework
upgrade compatibility checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in build_dkg_pvss_config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Remove unused lru dependency

The lru crate was only used for the fast path broadcast cache
(blocks_with_broadcasted_fast_shares) which has been removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Update Cargo.lock for lru removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 6 — render pipeline, captions, aspect ratio + face tracking

Renderer:
- ffmpeg_builder: filter_complex from TimelineModel (concat, xfade, title cards)
- preview_renderer: libx264 ultrafast, 1280px for fast scrubbing
- final_renderer: NVENC with auto-fallback to libx264 slow
- queue_manager: RenderJob state machine + ffprobe duration

Caption engine:
- generator: timeline-relative captions from TranscriptionSegments
- styler: ASS header + events, word_highlight karaoke tags (\\kf)
- platform presets: tiktok (Impact/72pt), youtube, instagram
- hex_to_ass_color: correct &HAABBGGRR channel reversal
- burner: standalone ASS burn-in for re-render after edit

Aspect ratio:
- detector: MediaPipe face detection with graceful import fallback
- reframer: center-crop or face-centered crop → ffmpeg_vf string
- tracker: temporal smoothing (rolling avg) for stable reframing

Routers: /api/render/* (6 endpoints), /api/captions/* (8 endpoints)
RenderJob.render_params column added (auto-migrated on startup)

commit.message:
fix: eliminar validaciones requeridas en carga de obras

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Launch Liri Vinyl Database — schema, contribution form, DB-first flip detection, flip notifications

Database (supabase/vinyl_schema.sql):
- vinyl_releases: one row per pressing/edition (catalog #, country, year,
  label, edition, version note, disc count, artwork, confirmed_count)
- vinyl_tracks: exact side positions (A1, A2, B1...) per release
- user_vinyl_collections: stub for future "tag your collection" feature
- RLS: public read, authenticated insert/update; auto updated_at trigger

add-vinyl.html (new page):
- 4-step flow: search iTunes → pressing details → side assignment → review/submit
- iTunes auto-populates artwork, tracks, year; user fills in pressing specifics
- Side assignment: ↑/↓ arrows move the FLIP divider between tracks per disc
- Multi-disc support: auto-groups by iTunes discNumber (LP1=A/B, LP2=C/D...)
- Writes to vinyl_releases + vinyl_tracks in Supabase on submit

index.html — vinyl mode upgrades:
- fetchVinylRelease: checks our DB first on every recognition
- getDbSideEndIndices: derives exact flip indices from DB track positions
- advanceToNextTrack now uses DB data > local learning > heuristic (priority order)
- "✓ In Liri's collection" badge shown when DB data is active
- Sides modal updated: shows "Not in Liri's collection yet" + link to add-vinyl.html
- Flip reminders settings: sound chime (Web Audio API) + push notification toggle
  - Sound: 3-note ascending chime via AudioContext (no file needed)
  - Push: Notification.requestPermission() with graceful denied state
- Both fire automatically when sideEndReason === "flip"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
